title: Variables & Output
description: Creating variables, choosing strings or numbers, adding comments, and printing variables with messages.

steps.title: Variables
steps.content:
Variables

A variable is a named box that stores a value so your program can use it later.

name = "Ryan"
age = 16

Use = to assign a value. Text values need quote marks. Number values do not.

String

city = "London"

Number

score = 100

Click run to see what happens

steps.title: Level 7 - Choose the Variable
steps.content:
Level 7 - Choose the Variable

Choose the correct Python code to create each variable.

Choose the correct variable creation:

steps.title: Typing Variables
steps.content:
Typing Variables

Now you will type the assignment yourself. The variable name goes on the left. The value goes on the right.

variable_name = "text value"
variable_name = 123

Click run to see what happens

Be precise: no spaces in the variable name, and quote marks only around strings.

steps.title: Level 8 - Create Variables
steps.content:
Level 8 - Create Variables

Read the comment and type the variable assignment.

Create the variable (e.g. name = "Bob"):

steps.title: Comments with Variables
steps.content:
Comments with Variables

A good comment can explain why a variable exists.

# Create variable name
name = "Ryan"

Click run to see what happens

For the next level, put the comment on line 1 and the variable code on line 2.

steps.title: Level 9 - Comment and Variable
steps.content:
Level 9 - Comment and Variable

Write an appropriate # comment, then create the variable on the next line.

Type comment & variable code (Enter for new line):

steps.title: Printing Variables
steps.content:
Printing Variables

Once a variable stores a value, you can print the variable name without quote marks.

name = "Ryan"
print(name)

Click run to see what happens

If you write print("name"), Python prints the word name. If you write print(name), Python prints the value stored in the variable.

steps.title: Level 10 - Print the Variable
steps.content:
Level 10 - Print the Variable

Create the variable, then print the variable on the next line.

Define variable & print it (Enter for new line):

steps.title: Printing Messages with Variables
steps.content:
Printing Messages with Variables

You can print text and a variable together by separating them with a comma.

city = "London"
print("I live in", city)

Click run to see what happens

The comma keeps the string and the variable separate, and Python adds a space in the output.

Choose the code that creates a variable and prints it with the correct message.

Define variable & print with message (e.g. print("Msg", var)):

steps.title: Stage 2 Ready
steps.content:
Ready for Stage 2

You have now practised every skill that appears in the Stage 2 exam.

Create variables

name = "Ryan"

Use numbers

score = 100

Add comments

# Create variable name

Print with messages

print("Name:", name)

Click run to see what happens
